title: ALP SoM hardware-revisions manifest (metadata/e1m_modules/<family>/hw-revisions.yaml) v1
description: Per-SoM-family hardware-revision compatibility table.  Each entry under `hw_revisions:` declares an rev label (e.g. r1, r2) with its SDK-version compatibility window + optional summary / changes bullets.  The customer-facing CHANGELOG.md alongside this file carries the full rev-by-rev narrative.

min_sdk_version.description: Lowest SDK version that supports this rev.  null = unbounded.

max_sdk_version.description: Highest SDK version that supports this rev.  null = unbounded (no deprecation yet).

board_rev.description: Altium board-design revision this SoM rev corresponds to (e.g. `R2`).  The SDK hw-rev key tracks it (rN == board RN).  The schematic/netlist export is labelled `<YYWW>-R<n>` (e.g. `2626-R2`, where `2626` is the YYWW date code = 2026 week 26, i.e. the `released` date) and lives privately under metadata/e1m_modules/<family>/netlists/ in alp-sdk-internal.

released.description: ISO date this rev started shipping.  Reserved-rev rows can omit.

summary.description: One-paragraph narrative of what defines this rev.

changes.description: Bullet list of changes from the previous rev (pin moves, chip swaps, RC bumps).  Customer-visible summary; deeper internal detail is held outside this tree.
